pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 15 creates file at this exact path; line 123 launches it; export_result.sh line 8 references it
setup_task.sh line 7 creates /home/ga/Diagrams/exports directory; export_result.sh line 9 defines this as EXPORT_PATH
setup_task.sh line 66 creates requirements document at this path with specific design pattern specifications

setup_task.sh lines 80-86 define Strategy Pattern requirements in refactoring_review.txt: PaymentStrategy interface with 3 concrete implementations (CreditCardPayment, PayPalPayment, BankTransferPayment)
setup_task.sh lines 88-94 define Observer Pattern requirements: OrderObserver interface with 3 concrete implementations (EmailNotifier, InventoryUpdater, AuditLogger)

setup_task.sh lines 96-100 define Factory Method Pattern requirements: ShippingProvider interface, ShippingProviderFactory abstract class, 2 concrete factories (DomesticShippingFactory, InternationalShippingFactory), and 2 concrete providers (FedExProvider, DHLProvider)

privileged_info_summary: This is a UML diagram refactoring task involving design patterns. The task operates on a synthetic 'God Class' diagram created at /home/ga/Diagrams/order_system.drawio that models an e-commerce order processing system with anti-patterns. The agent must refactor this diagram to apply three Gang of Four design patterns: Strategy Pattern (PaymentStrategy interface with CreditCardPayment, PayPalPayment, BankTransferPayment implementations), Observer Pattern (OrderObserver interface with EmailNotifier, InventoryUpdater, AuditLogger implementations), and Factory Method Pattern (ShippingProvider interface with ShippingProviderFactory abstract class, DomesticShippingFactory and InternationalShippingFactory concrete factories, and FedExProvider and DHLProvider concrete providers). Requirements are specified in /home/ga/Desktop/refactoring_review.txt. The refactored diagram must be exported as a PNG image to /home/ga/Diagrams/exports/order_system_refactored.png. All data is synthetic, created by task setup scripts.
